channel.title: SEAtoday - Food & Drink
channel.description: Upcoming events for SEAtoday - Food & Drink

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: Mahjong Monday and Martinis- Beginner 101 lesson
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Mercerwood Shore Club, 4150 E Mercer Way, Mercer Island, WA

Mondays and Mahjong are better with Martinis! Come learn American Mahjong with martinis, light snacks, and great company.

Event details

item.title: Raised By TV Events Presents: *ALL AGES* The Office Trivia Night
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Clock-Out Lounge, 4864 Beacon Ave S, Seattle, WA

RAISED By TV brings you:THE OFFICE Trivia Night!50 BRAND NEW questions about your favorite Scranton palsMon April 6th$7 ONLINE ADVANCE: \{plus ticketing fees\}$9 Day of & AT THE DOOR \{plus ticketing fees\}Doors open at 5:30. Trivia starts at 6:30!all ages til 8pm!Tables are first come first serve. \*\*PRIZES\*\*The RAISED BY TV gang has put together some killer prize packages. Teams of 1-5 are welcome. Prizes for 1st, 2nd, t have to be good at trivia just come on down to relive some Scranton shenanigans with us. \*\*FOOD & DRINKS\*\*The Clock-Out has bar and full Stevies Famous Pizza food service; one of the things that makes it our favorite place.The show is available on PeacockWe hope you can join RbyTV for a safe night of fun and brains.Brandon Ryan

Event details

item.title: Dragon Eye Paint and Sip at Jellyfish Brewing
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Jellyfish Brewing Company - Georgetown, 917 South Nebraska Street, Seattle, WA

Unleash your inner artist with a dragon eye painting while sipping brews at Jellyfish Brewing!

Event details

item.title: 5-Course Gil Family Estates Winemaker Dinner at 1 Hotel Seattle
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: 1 Hotel Seattle, 2125 Terry Avenue, Seattle, WA

Join the 1 Hotel Seattle team at La Loba for a special evening celebrating 110 years of Gil Family Estates with a five-course tasting menu and curated wine pairings. Guests will enjoy wines from across the Gil family portfolio—including selections from Bodegas El Nido, Shaya, and Juan Gil—presented by visiting winemakers and members of the Gil  Each course will be introduced by Chef Oscar and his culinary team, highlighting seasonal flavors designed to complement the wines. The evening begins with a welcome toast and continues through five paired courses in our Emerald Private Dining Room. Seating is communal to encourage conversation and connection among fellow wine lovers.

Event details

item.title: April Luncheon
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: Youngstown Cultural Arts Center, 4408 Delridge Wy SW, Seattle, WA

Description Explore new horizons and connect with fellow professionals at the West Seattle Chamber&'s monthly luncheons, where engaging discussions and networking opportunities lay the groundwork for you to grow your business.

Event details

Friday, April 10, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: Eileen's South Bellevue Studio, 4633 172nd Ave SE, Bellevue, WA

Join our friendly group for time, space, and help \(if needed\) to work on traditional and digital photo and craft projects.

item.title: Best of CID Food Tour
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Wing Luke Museum, 719 S. King Street, Seattle, WA

Best of CID Food Tour Friday, April 10 | 4:00PM-6:30PM General admission to the Museum is included with your tour ticket.

Event details

item.title: BRUNCH & BRUSH - MIMOSAS & ESPRESSO!
item.description:
Sunday, April 12, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Asylum Collective, 108 South Jackson Street B, Seattle, WA

Canvas and croissants, mimosas and mochas! Paint alongside artist Holly Turcotte in this playful, hands-on brunch experience.

Event details

item.title: Coworking Open House Week @ The Cloud Room
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: The Cloud Room, 1424 11th Ave Suite 400, Seattle, WA

Curious about coworking and looking for the perfect spot to get work done and reach your goals? Join us for a complimentary day during our upcoming Open House Week and experience what it’s like to be a part of our incredible community! What's Included: One Free Trial Day: Pick any day during Open House Week to try out our coworking space, free of charge! Arrive anytime 9am or later — whatever works best with your schedule. Access to our beautiful workspace including quiet desk space with monitors, large lounge area, phone booths, bar, and outdoor patio. Complimentary coffee, tea, snacks and kombucha. High-speed Wi-Fi, printing, and meeting rooms available for rent The chance to meet like-minded professionals and get a feel for the Cloud Room vibe. Special Offer: If you love your free day with us and decide to become a member, we’ll waive your initiation fees when you sign up! How to Join: Reserve your free trial day by selecting one day of your choice on our Calendly \(one free trial day per person\) Show up and enjoy your day at Cloud Room! Be sure to check in with the staff at the front of the lounge so we can give you a quick tour of the space and get you set up with the wifi. Sign up for membership to take advantage of waived initiation fees. Don’t miss this chance to test out our space, meet amazing people, and see if Cloud Room is the perfect fit for your work needs! Current Promotions: ⭐️ Sign up for a grounded membership and get your first month free \+ 10% off you first year of membership. 3 month commitment required. ⭐️ Floating Membership Promo: Take 50% off your first full month of floating membership. Spaces are limited—RSVP today.

Event details

item.title: Coworking Open House Week @ The Cloud Room
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 9 AM

Curious about coworking and looking for the perfect spot to get work done and reach your goals? Current Promotions: ⭐️ Sign up for a grounded membership and get your first month free \+ 10% off you first year of membership. 3 month commitment required. ⭐️ Floating Membership Promo: Take 50% off your first full month of floating membership. Spaces are limited—RSVP today.

Event details

item.title: Hill Talk: Stoup Brewing Capitol Hill
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Stoup Brewing, 1158 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Description HillTalk at Stoup Brewing \(Capitol Hill\) Tuesday, April 14  4:00-6:00 PM Join GSBA for our next HillTalk as we gather at Stoup Brewing's Capitol Hill taproom for a candid conversation about what's happening in our neighborhood and what it means for local businesses. HillTalk brings together GSBA members, business owners, community partners, and neighborhood leaders for real dialogue and practical updates. Whether you're navigating public safety concerns, city policy changes, foot traffic shifts, or new opportunities for collaboration, this is your space to stay informed and connected. Location: Stoup Brewing - Capitol Hill What is HillTalk? HillTalk is a casual, community-centered gathering where Capitol Hill businesses and community members can hear timely updates, ask questions, share perspective, and connect with the people shaping our neighborhood. What to Expect Neighborhood updates impacting Capitol Hill businesses Connection with fellow business owners and community partners A welcoming, relaxed setting to build relationships and stay engaged Come As You Are Grab a pint, pull up a chair, and be part of the conversation shaping Capitol Hill's future.

Event details

Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: Fogo De Chao, Alderwood Mall, Lynnwood, WA

Event details

item.title: HAPPY HOUR
item.description:
Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Description FREE Event for Lynnwood Chamber Members & their Guests - please register in advance. JANUARY HAPPY HOUR What better way to start the New Year than being in the presence of fitness, & healthy lifestyle options?? Join us Wednesday, January 29th at STRETCHLAB LYNNWOOD from 5:30 pm to 7:30 pm, where you'll get to mingle & network with other Chamber Members in an environment known for reducing stress, muscle & joint pain, improving posture & flexibility, and increasing our range of motion! This is event is FREE to all Chamber Members & their Guests, who register in advance! We're the Lynnwood Chamber of Commerce & we mean business...and networking!

Event details

item.title: Coffee Talk: The Sorrento Hotel
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Hotel Sorrento, 900 Madison Street, Seattle, WA

Description Join our Senior Membership Development Manager Alison for a casual hangout every third Thursday at a rotating coffee shop in Capitol Hill! No agenda, no specific topics; just an opportunity to meet fellow Capitol Hill business owners and members who live or work in our amazing neighborhood. Bring your laptop if you'd like to co-work, no big whoop! April's featured member: Hotel Sorrento STELLA/Fireside Room 900 Madison St,Seattle, WA 98104

Event details

item.title: Member Mixer
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: The Cloud Room, 1424 11th Ave Suite 400, Seattle, WA

Join us for our Member Mixer-a relaxed happy hour to connect, catch up, and raise a glass with fellow Cloud Room members! Whether you're new around here or a familiar face, this is a great chance to build community, spark a conversation, or just enjoy some downtime. We'll be pouring complimentary bubbles for members and their guests-swing by and say hey Open to members and their guests.

item.title: NCNW Seattle Pancake Breakfast Fundraiser
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: Applebee's Grill + Bar, 375 South Grady Way, Renton, WA

We're excited to announce our Summer Pancake Breakfast. Join us for a delicious meal while making a difference!

Event details

item.title: GU Earth Day Volunteering
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Duthie Hill Mountain Bike Park, Issaquah, WA

Zags—In honor of Earth Day, let’s get outside and give back! Join the Seattle Chapter for a community service day at Duthie Hill Mountain Bike Park in Sammamish on April 18, 10 AM–2 PM. We’ll be teaming up with Evergreen Mountain Bike Alliance to do trail maintenance at one of the region’s most-loved riding spots. No experience needed—just bring your energy, your Zag spirit, and a pair of work gloves. All tools will be provided by Evergreen, and we’ll have pizza and drinks ready for everyone. Kids are welcomed! Spots are limited—sign up here: https://www.evergreenmtb.org/index.php?option=com\_civicrm&task=civicrm/event/info&reset=1&id=5306 Let’s make an impact together. Go Zags!

Event details

item.title: Woodinville Market Fair: Spring April event
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Avallé Winemaking Studio & Watts Brewing Co, 15500 Woodinville Redmond Rd NE, Woodinville, WA

Spring is in the air, Seahawks spirit is everywhere, and the Woodinville Market Fair is bursting back to life! Join us on April 18th as we kick off a brand‑new season filled with incredible local vendors, delicious food trucks, raffle prizes, and all the warm, welcoming energy that makes our community shine. It’s the perfect way to celebrate fresh beginnings and the start of another unforgettable Woodinville Market Fair season. Both of our locations are family‑friendly spaces, perfect for spending a spring day together and celebrating fresh beginnings.Vendors interested in participating — we’d love to have you! Send us a message for details on how to join the lineup.

Event details

item.title: Earth Month Event | Restoration at Homestead Park
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 10 AM

Join us in Homestead Park where we will be removing common ivy and blackberry crowding native plants. This will be a great opportunity to learn the basics of restoration and plant identification, all while making our local forests healthier. Come get your hands in the dirt, work alongside neighbors and friends, and eat snacks! No experience necessary all ages welcome; gloves and tools are provided. Please note: we require participants aged 14 and under to volunteer with a parent or guardian at a ratio of 1 adult to 3 kids. When: Saturday, April 18th, 10am-1pm Where: Homestead Field, follow VOLUNTEER EVENT signs What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes you don't mind getting dirty \(think: long p ants, boots or sneakers\). Bring a water bottle. Small snacks, gloves, and tools will be provided. Registration: https://volunteersignup.org/RMPY L

Event details

item.title: The Sunday Slowdown Series: Breathe, Move, Brunch
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Salon Rae, 9734 Northeast 117th Lane, Kirkland, WA

Slow down your Sunday and reconnect with your body, breath, and community.

Event details
